text.title: Meta Ads Campaign Setup Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real ads-meta-campaign-setup engagements. Each pairs a symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: 20 ad sets in one campaign; none reaches 50 conv/wk; learning phase forever.
antipattern.root-cause: One ad set per audience permutation.
antipattern.fix: Collapse to 3-5 ad sets per campaign; if more audiences are needed, split into multiple campaigns.

antipattern.symptom: Campaign uses Reach objective; lots of impressions, zero purchases.
antipattern.root-cause: Objective copied from a previous awareness campaign.
antipattern.fix: Set objective to Conversions/Leads with the actual conversion event; never reuse objectives across funnel stages.

antipattern.symptom: Turned on CBO with only 12 conv/wk; one ad set hogs 90% of budget; the rest starve.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to CBO without checking conversion volume.
antipattern.fix: Stay on ABO until ≥50 conv/wk; explicitly enforce per-ad-set budget.

antipattern.symptom: Six ad sets named 'test1', 'test2', 'final', 'final-v2'. Reporting broken at scale.
antipattern.fix: Apply [stage]_[product]_[audience]_[creative]_[date] convention before any ad set is created.
